channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Control Resonant Early Access Window Confirmed for PS5 Exclusive
item.content:encoded:
Remedy’s Control Resonant is receiving a PS5-exclusive early access period.

Control Resonant received a release date during the PlayStation State of Play presentation on June 2, 2026. The game will release worldwide on September 24 for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC. However, PlayStation players who pre-order the Deluxe Edition will be able to play slightly earlier.

The official PlayStation blog post covering the game’s latest information revealed a 48-hour Early Access window that is not present for PC players. Additionally, Thomas Puha, a Remedy executive, confirmed that the early access grace period is exclusive to PlayStation.

Pre-orders for Control Resonant are available today. The PlayStation 5 Digital Deluxe Edition includes 48 hours of advance access.

Backlash to Control Resonant Launching Early on PS5 Before Other Platforms

In some of the immediate responses to Puha, players who pre-ordered the game on other platforms expressed their displeasure and talked of canceling their orders.

As for the plot, this will be a follow-up to the 2019 game. This time around, players will follow Dylan Faden, and, much like in the first game, while his story drives the main plot, his sibling will shape the path. As the official blog describes, “The Fadens’ arcs revolve around each other, and that’s what propels their shared story. Both games are, ultimately, about the siblings confronting their destinies. Dylan’s journey is shaped by Jesse’s actions as humanity meets a new existential threat. She remains central and has a presence in the game, but he’s the one moving the story forward.”

To learn more about everything that was announced during the recent State of Play, click here. Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Jojopyun, MKOI, and KCorp All Fined Following LEC Roadtrip
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has sanctioned two of its LEC teams in Movistar KOI and Karmine Corp, as well as Canadian midlaner Joseph Joon ‘Jojopyun’ Pyun.

As added to Riot Games’ EMEA penalty tracker, all three were handed warnings and four-figure fines.

ing the LEC Roadtrip in Madrid a month ago.

The punishments handed out were:

  • Movistar KOI — Warning + €5,000 fine
  • Karmine Corp — Warning + €5,000 fine
  • Jojopyun (Movistar KOI) — Warning + €2,000 fine

Reasons for the LEC Fines

Both organisations’ fines were due to the actions of their content creator owners, MKOI’s Ibai ‘Ibai’ Llanos Garatea and KCorp’s Kamel ‘Kameto’ Kebir.

The two were supposed to face off in a showmatch in Madrid, which was highly anticipated by both sets of fans.

However, public arguments between the two led to the match’s cancellation.

French streamer Jean ‘TraYtoN’ Medzadourian stoked the flames of the tense French and Spanish rivalry by insulting the low fan turnout during the Friday afternoon of the Madrid event.

This then led to Ibai insulting the KCorp fanbase and Kameto responding by cancelling his flight to Spain.

Meanwhile, Jojopyun was fined and handed his second warning in a year for swearing during an interview, a moment the LEC’s own Twitter account shared.

After tying up the tense series against KCOrp, Jojopyun told LEC interviewer Laure Valée, “We are gonna f*ck KC so hard the next game.”

Meanwhile, in the game before, KCorp ADC Caliste ‘Caliste’ Henry-Hennebert stood up midmatch to take off his headset and send a shh gesture to his opponents.

However, the young Frenchman avoided any punishment.

Do you think these warnings and punishments are fair, while LEC fans ask for more personality? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

In other LEC drama, it appears Fnatic will drop toplaner Panagiotis ‘Empyros’ Tantis and search for a Korean replacement.

MOBILE APP
